symbols.name: n
symbols.def: Number of units in the finite population.

symbols.name: m_n
symbols.def: Number of ego-clusters randomized at stage n.

symbols.name: U_n
symbols.def: Unit index set U_n = {1, ..., n}.

symbols.name: W_n
symbols.def: Ego-cluster treatment vector W_n = (W_cn)_{c=1}^{m_n}.

symbols.name: p_n
symbols.def: Bernoulli treatment probability for each ego-cluster.

symbols.name: H_n
symbols.def: Finite set of analyst-specified exposure labels.

symbols.name: h_n^+
symbols.def: Treated exposure label in the target contrast.

symbols.name: h_n^-
symbols.def: Reference exposure label in the target contrast.

symbols.name: Y_in(h)
symbols.def: Potential outcome for unit i in stage n under exposure label h.

symbols.name: Y_in^obs
symbols.def: Observed outcome for unit i in stage n under the realized exposure label.

symbols.name: M_ihn
symbols.def: Family of signed cluster-index monomials defining exposure h for unit i at stage n.

symbols.name: P_ihn(W_n)
symbols.def: Incidence polynomial whose positivity defines exposure label h for unit i.

symbols.name: E_ihn
symbols.def: Indicator that W_n places unit i in exposure label h.

symbols.name: pi_ihn
symbols.def: pi_ihn = Pr_pi(E_ihn = 1).

symbols.name: pi_ij(h,h',n)
symbols.def: pi_ij(h,h',n) = Pr_pi(E_ihn = 1, E_jh'n = 1).

symbols.name: B_ihn
symbols.def: Set of ego-cluster indices appearing in at least one monomial in M_ihn.

symbols.name: B_in
symbols.def: Contrast support B_in = B_ih_n^+n union B_ih_n^-n.

symbols.name: chi(A)
symbols.def: chi(A) equals one when the union of signed literals in A contains no cluster index with both signs.

symbols.name: u(A)
symbols.sig: finite collections of signed monomials -> N
symbols.def: Number of distinct cluster indices that appear with sign +1 in the consistent union of A.

symbols.name: v(A)
symbols.sig: finite collections of signed monomials -> N
symbols.def: Number of distinct cluster indices that appear with sign -1 in the consistent union of A.

symbols.name: q_n
symbols.def: Maximum cardinality of any signed monomial in any M_ihn used by the target contrast.

symbols.name: kappa_n
symbols.def: Maximum number of signed monomials in any M_ihn used by the target contrast.

symbols.name: omega_n
symbols.def: omega_n = max_c |{ i in U_n : c in B_in }|, the largest number of target-contrast units whose exposure supports involve the same ego-cluster c.

symbols.name: b_n
symbols.def: b_n = max_i |B_in|.

symbols.name: M_Y
symbols.def: Uniform bound on absolute potential outcomes.

symbols.name: pi_min
symbols.def: Lower bound on target exposure probabilities pi_ih_n^+n and pi_ih_n^-n.

symbols.name: c_sigma
symbols.def: Positive constant lower bounding n sigma_n^2.

symbols.name: tau_n
symbols.def: tau_n = n^(-1) sum_{i=1}^n { Y_in(h_n^+) - Y_in(h_n^-) }.

symbols.name: hat_tau_HT_n
symbols.def: Horvitz-Thompson estimator for tau_n using the two target exposure labels and their known design probabilities.

symbols.name: psi_in
symbols.def: psi_in = 1{E_ih_n^+n = 1} Y_in^obs / pi_ih_n^+n - 1{E_ih_n^-n = 1} Y_in^obs / pi_ih_n^-n.

symbols.name: G_n^dep
symbols.def: Graph whose vertices are units and whose edges connect i and j whenever B_in and B_jn intersect.

symbols.name: Delta_n
symbols.def: Maximum degree of G_n^dep.

symbols.name: sigma_n^2
symbols.def: sigma_n^2 = Var_pi(hat_tau_HT_n).

symbols.name: alpha
symbols.def: Nominal miscoverage level for Wald intervals.

symbols.name: CI_n^orc(1-alpha)
symbols.def: CI_n^orc(1-alpha) = [hat_tau_HT_n - z_(1-alpha/2) sigma_n, hat_tau_HT_n + z_(1-alpha/2) sigma_n].

assumptions.condition: The ego-cluster assignments W_1n, ..., W_m_nn are mutually independent Bernoulli(p_n) random variables.

standard.name: Bernoulli treatment assignment specialized to ego-clusters

assumptions.condition: For every i in U_n, the observed outcome satisfies Y_in^obs = sum_{h in H_n} 1{E_ihn = 1} Y_in(h), and at most one label h has E_ihn = 1.
max_{i in U_n, h in H_n} |Y_in(h)| <= M_Y.
min_{i in U_n} { pi_ih_n^+n, pi_ih_n^-n } >= pi_min.
Delta_n / n^(1/4) -> 0 as n -> infinity (equivalently Delta_n = o(n^(1/4))).

novel.justification: The CLT needs the induced ego-overlap dependency degree to satisfy Delta_n=o(n^(1/4)); prop:dependency-degree gives the primitive sufficient screen Delta_n <= b_n(omega_n-1) <= 2 q_n kappa_n(omega_n-1), so analysts may verify the stronger support-level bound when convenient.

assumptions.condition: For any subclass C of def:clt-qualified-ego-incidence-array-class over which uniform coverage is claimed, rho_n^Delta(C):=sup_{array in C} Delta_n(array) / n^(1/4) -> 0 as n -> infinity.

novel.justification: standard: uniform version of a pointwise asymptotic sparsity condition used to convert a pointwise Berry-Stein bound into a uniform CLT. A sufficient support-level screen is sup_{array in C} b_n(array)(omega_n(array)-1)/n^(1/4) -> 0, via the prop:dependency-degree chain.

definitions.name: Positive-probability ego-incidence Bernoulli arrays
definitions.construction: { ((Y_in(h))_{i in U_n, h in H_n}, (W_n)_{n>=1}, (M_ihn)_{i in U_n, h in {h_n^+, h_n^-}}) : ass:bernoulli-ego-design and ass:exposure-consistency and ass:bounded-outcomes and ass:positivity }

definitions.name: CLT-qualified positive-probability ego-incidence Bernoulli arrays
definitions.construction: { ((Y_in(h))_{i in U_n, h in H_n}, (W_n)_{n>=1}, (M_ihn)_{i in U_n, h in {h_n^+, h_n^-}}) : ass:bernoulli-ego-design and ass:exposure-consistency and ass:bounded-outcomes and ass:positivity and ass:variance-nondegenerate and ass:primitive-degree-rate }

definitions.name: Signed cluster literal
definitions.construction: For each cluster index c and sign sigma in {-1,1}, L_csigma_n(W_n) = W_cn if sigma = 1 and L_csigma_n(W_n) = 1 - W_cn if sigma = -1.

definitions.name: Signed incidence polynomial
definitions.construction: P_ihn(W_n) = sum_{A in M_ihn} prod_{(c,sigma) in A} L_csigma_n(W_n).

definitions.name: Literal consistency indicator
definitions.construction: chi(A) = 1 if no cluster index c appears with both signs +1 and -1 in the union of the signed monomials in A, and chi(A) = 0 otherwise.

definitions.name: Treated literal count
definitions.construction: u(A) = |{ c : (c,1) appears in the union of A and (c,-1) does not appear in the union of A }|.

definitions.name: Control literal count
definitions.construction: v(A) = |{ c : (c,-1) appears in the union of A and (c,1) does not appear in the union of A }|.

definitions.name: Horvitz-Thompson exposure contrast
definitions.construction: hat_tau_HT_n = n^(-1) sum_{i=1}^n [ 1{E_ih_n^+n = 1} Y_in^obs / pi_ih_n^+n - 1{E_ih_n^-n = 1} Y_in^obs / pi_ih_n^-n ].

definitions.name: Ego-overlap dependency graph
definitions.construction: G_n^dep has vertex set U_n and edge set { {i,j} : i != j and B_in intersect B_jn is nonempty }.

definitions.name: Oracle Wald interval
definitions.construction: CI_n^orc(1-alpha) = [hat_tau_HT_n - z_(1-alpha/2) sigma_n, hat_tau_HT_n + z_(1-alpha/2) sigma_n].

definitions.name: Dual upper-envelope variance handle
definitions.construction: Let R={+,-}, h_+=h_n^+, h_-=h_n^-, s_+=1, s_-=-1, pi_{ir}=pi_{i h_r n}, and E_{ir}=E_{i h_r n}. For i!=j set pi_{ij}^{rs}=pi_ij(h_r,h_s,n); for i=j set pi_{ii}^{rr}=pi_{ir} and pi_{ii}^{rs}=Pr_pi(E_{ir}=1,E_{is}=1), equal to 0 for distinct labels under exposure consistency and to pi_{ir} if the two labels coincide. Define Gamma_{ij}^{rs}=pi_{ij}^{rs}/(pi_{ir}pi_{js})-1 and Q_n(y)=n^{-2} sum_{i,j} sum_{r,s in R} s_r s_s Gamma_{ij}^{rs} y_{ir} y_{js}. Given realized exposure indicators and outcomes, let F_n^{obs}={y in [-M_Y,M_Y]^{2n}: E_{ir}=1 implies y_{ir}=Y_in^obs for every i,r}. Set Vbar_n=max_{y in F_n^{obs}} Q_n(y).

statements.statement: Under ass:bernoulli-ego-design, for every unit i in U_n and target exposure label h in {h_n^+, h_n^-}, pi_ihn = sum_{emptyset != A subseteq M_ihn} (-1)^(|A|+1) chi(A) p_n^(u(A)) (1-p_n)^(v(A)). For every pair i != j and labels h, h' in {h_n^+, h_n^-}, pi_ij(h,h',n) = sum_{emptyset != A subseteq M_ihn} sum_{emptyset != B subseteq M_jh'n} (-1)^(|A|+|B|) chi(A union B) p_n^(u(A union B)) (1-p_n)^(v(A union B)).
statements.justification: Exact HT weighting and any design-based variance calculation require first and joint exposure probabilities, and the gap is algebraic rather than conceptual.
statements.gap: UganderKarrerBackstromKleinberg2013 gives exact probabilities for selected neighborhood-exposure conditions under graph-cluster randomization, but not a closed signed-incidence polynomial formula for ego-cluster exposure labels with both treated and control literals.
statements.consumer: This proposition turns analyst-chosen ego exposure rules into computable HT weights and computable joint probability inputs for the downstream CLT and variance question.

statements.statement: Under ass:bernoulli-ego-design and ass:exposure-consistency, let G_n^dep be as in def:dependency-graph. Then the centered raw summands psi_in - E_pi[psi_in] admit G_n^dep as a dependency graph, and Delta_n <= b_n (omega_n - 1) <= 2 q_n kappa_n (omega_n - 1).
statements.justification: The primitive challenge is to derive the dependency graph from overlap counts in the ego design rather than to assume one abstractly.
statements.gap: Chin2018 works with a restricted-degree dependency graph supplied as an assumption; this proposition derives the graph and its degree bound from exposure supports B_in, with the extra factor two coming from the union of the treated-label and reference-label supports inside each contrast support.
statements.consumer: Experiment designers can screen whether a proposed ego partition makes Gaussian inference plausible before computing any outcomes.

statements.statement: Under ass:bernoulli-ego-design, ass:exposure-consistency, ass:bounded-outcomes, ass:positivity, ass:variance-nondegenerate, and ass:primitive-degree-rate, (hat_tau_HT_n - tau_n) / sigma_n converges in distribution to N(0,1). Consequently, for every fixed alpha in (0,1), Pr_pi{ tau_n in CI_n^orc(1-alpha) } -> 1 - alpha.
statements.justification: The theorem reduces fixed-potential-outcome ego-cluster exposure contrasts from signed-incidence ego-cluster exposure supports to an overlap-derived dependency graph, uses the exact inclusion-exclusion algebra for first and joint target-exposure probabilities, and applies an existing dependency-graph Stein CLT under Delta_n=o(n^(1/4)).
statements.gap: Relative to Aronow-Samii and Chin, the theorem's contribution is the explicit reduction from signed-incidence ego-cluster exposure supports to an overlap-derived dependency graph with an exact inclusion-exclusion algebra for the first and joint target-exposure probabilities, yielding a design-based Horvitz-Thompson CLT and oracle Wald coverage under a growing dependency degree Delta_n=o(n^(1/4))--strictly beyond the constant/high-level-dependence regime of Aronow-Samii (2017) and the restricted fixed-degree regime of Chin. It should not be described as the first design-based inference object or as a distinct oracle-Wald framework.
statements.consumer: The theorem supplies the primary kernel for exact HT exposure contrasts in ego-cluster experiments: computable exposure probabilities, an overlap-derived dependency graph, and oracle-valid Wald intervals that feed the feasible observed-data envelope.

statements.statement: For every realized assignment and observed target-exposure dataset generated by an admissible finite-population array, the dual upper-envelope handle of def:variance-envelope-handle satisfies Vbar_n >= sigma_n^2 (pathwise).
statements.justification: The realized bounded target-potential completion is feasible for F_n^obs, so the envelope maximum dominates the realized design variance.
statements.gap: This records the pathwise completion argument needed by the feasible Wald theorem instead of leaving it implicit in proof prose.

statements.statement: Given the realized exposure indicators and outcomes, F_n^obs is a finite-dimensional box with equality constraints and Vbar_n = max_{y in F_n^obs} Q_n(y) is an observed-data functional whose maximum is attained and computable from observed-population inputs.
statements.justification: The feasible set is a closed bounded finite-dimensional polytope and Q_n uses only observed outcomes and design probabilities.
statements.gap: This makes the existence and computability of the variance-envelope optimizer explicit for the solver.

statements.statement: Vbar_n is the pointwise smallest observed-data upper envelope for sigma_n^2 over all bounded target-potential completions consistent with the realized target-exposure data.
statements.justification: Since Vbar_n is the supremum of Q_n over the completion class, any observed-data upper envelope over that class must be at least Vbar_n.
statements.gap: This isolates the theorem's sharpness claim within the observed-data variance-envelope family.

statements.statement: Under ass:bernoulli-ego-design, ass:exposure-consistency, ass:bounded-outcomes, ass:positivity, ass:variance-nondegenerate, and ass:uniform-primitive-degree-rate, for any subclass C of def:clt-qualified-ego-incidence-array-class over which uniform coverage is claimed, liminf_n inf_{A in C} Pr_{pi,A}{ |hat_tau_HT_n - tau_n| <= z_(1-alpha/2) sigma_n(A) } >= 1 - alpha (uniform oracle Wald coverage).
statements.justification: The same dependency-graph Stein bound used pointwise becomes uniform after taking the subclass supremum of the degree-rate remainder.
statements.gap: This separates uniform oracle Wald coverage from thm:ego-overlap-clt so the feasible theorem has an explicit uniform coverage input.

statements.statement: Under ass:bernoulli-ego-design, ass:exposure-consistency, ass:bounded-outcomes, ass:positivity, ass:variance-nondegenerate, and ass:primitive-degree-rate, with Vbar_n the computable functional of def:variance-envelope-handle, for every fixed alpha in (0,1), liminf_n Pr_pi{ tau_n in [hat_tau_HT_n - z_(1-alpha/2) sqrt(Vbar_n), hat_tau_HT_n + z_(1-alpha/2) sqrt(Vbar_n)] } >= 1 - alpha for each sequence in def:clt-qualified-ego-incidence-array-class, and uniformly over any subclass satisfying ass:uniform-primitive-degree-rate. Moreover Vbar_n is the pointwise smallest observed-data upper envelope for sigma_n^2 over bounded target-potential completions consistent with the realized target-exposure data, so this feasible Wald interval is the sharp conservative one in that class.
statements.justification: The feasible conservative variance is a sharp complementary add-on to the primary CLT kernel: because Vbar_n >= sigma_n^2 pathwise for the fixed HT contrast, the oracle confidence interval is contained in the feasible one, so coverage transfers from thm:ego-overlap-clt by monotonicity with no extra assumption; sharpness is only within the observed-data variance-envelope Wald family.
statements.gap: Harshaw-Middleton-Savje (2112.01709) optimize over the class of quadratic-form variance estimators to obtain the minimally-conservative estimable upper bound of the unknown design variance. The feasible-Wald envelope here is a different optimization: for the fixed Horvitz-Thompson contrast variance, Vbar_n = max_{y in F_n^obs} Q_n(y) is the sharp upper envelope over the unknown bounded target-potential completions consistent with the realized exposure data. The two optimize over different domains (estimator class vs completion class), so the completion-sharp envelope is complementary to, not a special case of, the HMS estimator-optimization program. The theorem does not claim global minimal conservativeness over all variance procedures or all conservative intervals; it claims pointwise shortest Wald half-length only within the observed-data variance-envelope Wald family for the fixed HT contrast.
statements.consumer: The theorem turns the oracle CLT into an operational confidence interval for platform ego experiments on the same bounded-overlap class, as a completion-sharp add-on to the signed-incidence dependency-graph kernel and without importing model-based regression assumptions.

statements.statement: If q_n = 1, kappa_n = 1, and for every i and h in {h_n^+,h_n^-} the family M_ihn consists of exactly one nonempty singleton signed monomial, then each target exposure event is a single signed literal, b_n <= 2, pi_ihn = p_n^(u_i(h)) (1-p_n)^(v_i(h)), Delta_n <= 2 (omega_n - 1), and thm:ego-overlap-clt reduces to a standard dependency-graph HT CLT for a bounded-degree exposure map.
statements.justification: A credible new kernel should collapse to the known local-dependence benchmark when the exposure map becomes a single literal.
statements.gap: This reduction pins the proposal to the Chin2018 and AronowSamii2017 benchmark rather than claiming a wholly new asymptotic regime.
statements.consumer: The lemma is the sanity check that makes the general signed-polynomial theorem interpretable to referees.

estimand_functional: hat_tau_HT_n = n^(-1) sum_{i=1}^n [ 1{E_ih_n^+n = 1} Y_in^obs / pi_ih_n^+n - 1{E_ih_n^-n = 1} Y_in^obs / pi_ih_n^-n ]

sampling_model.design: Finite-population ego-cluster Bernoulli randomization: cluster indicators W_1n, ..., W_m_nn are independent Bernoulli(p_n).
sampling_model.units: Potential outcomes {Y_in(h): i in U_n, h in H_n} are fixed; randomness comes only from W_n.
sampling_model.treatment: Analyst-chosen exposure labels h are induced by signed incidence polynomials P_ihn(W_n) over ego-cluster treatment indicators.
sampling_model.overlap: Dependence is summarized by support sets B_in, maximal support size b_n, and maximal ego-overlap multiplicity omega_n.
sampling_model.nuisance_estimation: None; pi_ihn and pi_ij(h,h',n) are design probabilities derived from prop:incidence-probabilities rather than estimated nonparametrically.

bibliography.citation: Aronow, Peter M., and Cyrus Samii (2017). "Estimating Average Causal Effects Under General Interference, with Application to a Social Network Experiment." Annals of Applied Statistics 11(4): 1912-1947.
Ugander, Johan, Brian Karrer, Lars Backstrom, and Jon Kleinberg (2013). "Graph Cluster Randomization: Network Exposure to Multiple Universes." Proceedings of KDD 2013.
Chin, Alex (2018). "Central Limit Theorems via Stein's Method for Randomized Experiments under Interference." arXiv:1804.03105.
Leung, Michael P. (2019). "Causal Inference Under Approximate Neighborhood Interference." arXiv:1911.07085.
Gao, Mengsi, and Peng Ding (2023). "Causal Inference in Network Experiments: Regression-Based Analysis and Design-Based Properties." arXiv:2309.07476.
Chattopadhyay, Ambarish, Kosuke Imai, and Jose R. Zubizarreta (2023). "Design-Based Inference for Generalized Network Experiments with Stochastic Interventions." arXiv:2312.03268.
Kung, Kelly, and Daniel L. Sussman (2022). "Unbiased Estimation for Additive Exposure Models." arXiv:2209.01053.

Harshaw, Christopher, Joel A. Middleton, and Fredrik Sävje (2026). "Optimized Variance Estimation under Interference and Complex Experimental Designs." Journal of the American Statistical Association. arXiv:2112.01709.
Sävje, Fredrik, Peter M. Aronow, and Michael G. Hudgens (2021). "Average Treatment Effects in the Presence of Unknown Interference." Annals of Statistics 49(2): 673-701.
Aronow, Peter M., Dean Eckles, Cyrus Samii, and Stephanie Zonszein (2021). "Spillover Effects in Experimental Data." In Handbook of Research Methods in Health Social Sciences.
Liu, Xiao, Feifang Hu, and Jingfei Zhang (2026). "Estimating Treatment and Spillover Effects with the Ego-Cluster Experimental Design." arXiv:2605.00534.

comparator_promise_table.comparator_claim: Graph cluster randomization yields exact exposure probabilities for selected network-exposure conditions and variance guarantees under restricted-growth graphs.
Exposure-mapping Horvitz-Thompson contrasts admit randomization-based inference once first and joint exposure probabilities are known, with asymptotic normality under local dependence.
Stein's method delivers central limit theorems for randomized experiments under interference when the dependency graph degree is suitably restricted.
Ego-cluster randomization yields model-based consistency and asymptotic normality with asymptotic variances driven by ego-cluster structure.
Regression-based network-robust covariance estimators can be anti-conservative, motivating adjusted covariance estimation in network experiments.

Optimized variance estimation under interference and complex experimental designs obtains the minimally-conservative estimable upper bound of the unknown design variance by optimizing over the class of quadratic-form variance estimators.

tldr: The proposal studies finite-population Horvitz-Thompson exposure contrasts in ego-cluster experiments when analyst-defined exposure labels are encoded by signed incidence polynomials over cluster treatment indicators. The field-level contribution is an explicit reduction from signed-incidence ego-cluster exposure supports to an overlap-derived dependency graph with an exact inclusion-exclusion algebra for the first and joint target-exposure probabilities, yielding a design-based Horvitz-Thompson CLT and oracle Wald coverage under a growing dependency degree Delta_n=o(n^(1/4))--strictly beyond the constant/high-level-dependence regime of Aronow-Samii (2017) and the restricted fixed-degree regime of Chin. The feasible conservative Wald interval is a sharp complementary add-on built on that kernel: for the fixed HT contrast, Vbar_n = max_{y in F_n^obs} Q_n(y) is completion-sharp and pointwise shortest within the observed-data variance-envelope Wald family. Harshaw-Middleton-Savje (2112.01709) instead optimize over the class of quadratic-form variance estimators to obtain the minimally-conservative estimable upper bound of the unknown design variance, so the two optimizations are complementary rather than nested.

project_justification.gap: The literature has either the exposure-mapping HT architecture without ego-cluster probability algebra, generic interference CLTs without an ego-cluster reduction to an overlap-derived dependency graph, model-based ego-cluster asymptotics without fixed-potential-outcome HT contrasts, or estimator-class conservative-variance programs that optimize a different object. The missing kernel is an explicit reduction from signed-incidence ego-cluster exposure supports to an overlap-derived dependency graph with an exact inclusion-exclusion algebra for the first and joint target-exposure probabilities, yielding a design-based Horvitz-Thompson CLT and oracle Wald coverage under a growing dependency degree Delta_n=o(n^(1/4))--strictly beyond the constant/high-level-dependence regime of Aronow-Samii (2017) and the restricted fixed-degree regime of Chin.
project_justification.niche: This opens a design-based inference niche for platform-style ego experiments where the analyst wants exact exposure contrasts under user-defined local rules rather than regression coefficients under a working model.
project_justification.fill: The project fills the gap by deriving exact first and joint exposure probabilities for signed incidence-polynomial labels, translating the same exposure supports into an explicit overlap-derived dependency graph with growing-degree condition Delta_n=o(n^(1/4)), proving the design-based HT CLT and oracle Wald coverage on that kernel, and adding a computable completion-sharp variance envelope as a sharp complementary observed-data Wald add-on.

related_work: Aronow and Samii (2017) provide the exposure-mapping Horvitz-Thompson architecture, randomization-based variance decomposition, and local-dependence asymptotics that this proposal keeps, but they leave the exposure probabilities and the dependence neighborhood abstract; the present contribution is the explicit reduction from signed-incidence ego-cluster exposure supports to an overlap-derived dependency graph with exact inclusion-exclusion algebra for first and joint target-exposure probabilities, yielding a design-based HT CLT and oracle Wald coverage under growing dependency degree Delta_n=o(n^(1/4)), strictly beyond their constant/high-level-dependence regime. Ugander, Karrer, Backstrom, and Kleinberg (2013) compute exact probabilities for several graph-cluster exposure conditions and show that suitable clustering can sharply improve variance, but they do not give a signed incidence-polynomial algebra for ego-cluster exposure labels. Chin (2018) proves Stein-method central limit theorems under a restricted fixed-degree dependency condition, whereas the present proposal derives the graph from the contrast support geometry B_in and the overlap index omega_n, then assumes the consumed growing-degree rate Delta_n=o(n^(1/4)). Leung (2019) moves to approximate neighborhood interference and network HAC inference in a superpopulation framework, which is complementary but not a fixed finite-population HT Wald result for analyst-specified exposure labels. Chattopadhyay, Imai, and Zubizarreta (2023) develop design-based unbiased estimation and conservative variance estimators for stochastic-intervention estimands in generalized network experiments, but their estimand class differs from fixed exposure-label HT contrasts and does not supply the ego-incidence probability algebra or the same observed-data variance-envelope Wald family. Kung and Sussman (2022) characterize linear unbiased estimators under additive exposure models and identify optimal affine-basis estimators, but the additive restriction changes the estimand class and does not answer the nonadditive conservative-envelope question for Horvitz-Thompson exposure contrasts. Harshaw-Middleton-Savje (2112.01709) optimize over the class of quadratic-form variance estimators to obtain the minimally-conservative estimable upper bound of the unknown design variance. The feasible-Wald envelope here is a different optimization: for the fixed Horvitz-Thompson contrast variance, Vbar_n = max_{y in F_n^obs} Q_n(y) is the sharp upper envelope over the unknown bounded target-potential completions consistent with the realized exposure data. The two optimize over different domains (estimator class vs completion class), so the completion-sharp envelope is complementary to, not a special case of, the HMS estimator-optimization program. Gao and Ding (2023) show that regression-based network-robust covariances can be anti-conservative, which is why the proposal keeps the CLT and the conservative variance scope separate. Liu, Hu, and Zhang (2026) are the closest ego-cluster comparator, but their estimators and asymptotics are model-based and do not deliver exact exposure-weighted design-based inference.
honest_scope: Settled target: exact first and joint exposure probabilities for the signed incidence-polynomial class, an overlap-derived dependency graph with growing-degree condition Delta_n=o(n^(1/4)) and sufficient screens b_n(omega_n-1)=o(n^(1/4)) or 2 q_n kappa_n(omega_n-1)=o(n^(1/4)), yielding a design-based Horvitz-Thompson CLT and oracle Wald coverage beyond the constant/high-level-dependence regime of Aronow-Samii (2017) and the restricted fixed-degree regime of Chin. The feasible conservative Wald interval is a sharp complementary add-on: for the fixed HT contrast, Vbar_n = max_{y in F_n^obs} Q_n(y) is a computable completion-sharp variance envelope delivering at-least-nominal coverage only within the observed-data variance-envelope Wald family. Harshaw-Middleton-Savje (2112.01709) optimize over quadratic-form variance estimators rather than bounded target-potential completions, so this proposal is complementary to their estimator-optimization program. The proposal deliberately does not claim to be a first design-based inference object, a new general conservative-variance framework, globally minimally conservative over all intervals, optimal for HT relative to alternative linear estimators, adaptive ego-cluster randomization, or a general design problem over all clusterings.
